summary: Patrick McKenzie notes that by mid-March 2007, his software business profits have already exceeded all of 2006. He projects March will be his best month ever, with sales between $800-$1,000 against roughly $150 in expenses. This very brief post captures an early milestone in the growth of Bingo Card Creator, his first software product.
key_points: By March 13, 2007, cumulative profits for the year had already surpassed all of 2006
Projected monthly sales of $800-$1,000 with ~$150 in expenses, showing strong margins
Physical CD sales were still a meaningful cost driver at this early stage of the business
money_quotes: Its March 13th in Japan and my profits for 2007 just exceeded my profits for 2006.
March is shaping up to be my best month ever.
key_insight: Even at modest absolute numbers, rapid year-over-year growth in a solo software business signals compounding momentum.
